name: Concentration Curl
resistance_profile: Hardest through the top half of the curl — the forward-leaning, thigh-braced upper arm keeps the dumbbell's moment arm long as you squeeze toward the shoulder, while the straight-down bottom hang sits nearly unloaded.
cues: Elbow braced on the inner thigh
Curl with a hard peak squeeze
execution_steps: Sit on a bench, lean forward, and brace the back of your working upper arm against your inner thigh.
Let the dumbbell hang straight down with the arm fully extended.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder, squeezing the biceps hard at the top.
Lower slowly back to a full stretch without letting the elbow move.
good_when: You want a strict single-arm movement with a strong peak contraction.
You struggle to feel the biceps working and need to remove momentum.
bad_when: You want to move heavy loads for overall mass (use a barbell or dumbbell curl).
You want a stretch-position emphasis (use an incline curl).
